description: Source-module size governance ledger. Enforced by scripts/check_source_module_size.py (wired into scripts/run_drift_gates.sh). Any tracked source module (crates/**/src/**.rs, scripts/*.py, mcp-server/**.py) over threshold_lines FAILS the gate UNLESS it is registered here as an oversized module with status 'decomposition-pending', an honest recorded_lines, and a FROZEN ceiling_lines. This makes known-oversized modules visibly FLAGGED and budgeted (not silently tolerated) while NEW oversized modules fail immediately. Decomposition is governance-triggered and organic (docs/DECOMPOSITION_PLAN.md / Decomposition Philosophy) — registration here is the governance flag, NOT a schedule to split. The gate also fails if a recorded_lines drifts materially from the live count (the ledger cannot rot) and warns when a registered module shrinks back under threshold (de-list candidate). GROWTH CEILING (perpetuation guard): each entry carries a frozen ceiling_lines = its registered baseline + headroom (growth_headroom: the larger of growth_headroom_floor lines or growth_headroom_pct of the baseline). A flagged module may grow only up to its ceiling; past it the gate FAILS — the ONLY sanctioned way back under the ceiling is to DECOMPOSE (which shrinks the file), never to raise the ceiling. This stops a registered monolith growing without bound.

crates/cli/src/main_tests/main_tests_pro.note: CLI integration-test module (board component pool materialization).

crates/gui-app/src/main.rs.note: winit GUI shell entry + app loop. Decomposition in progress: camera/pane/dock-resize view ops extracted to runtime_camera_pane.rs (decision 021 / source-size governance); ceiling ratcheted down with the shrink.

crates/gui-protocol/src/lib.rs.note: GUI scene-contract + primitive types (no GUI deps). Still oversized; the workspace consumer-state cluster was extracted to workspace_layout.rs (decision 021).
